summary: Cadastral survey of the sacred precinct of Pachacamac -- the only substantial text khipu recovered from the site. 212 cords, monochrome brown (209/212), 10 sections. siqa (to climb/ramp) x5 = the piramides con rampa that define Pachacamac's architecture. qaqa (rock/temple platform) x13 = dominant word. Section 2 header [17, 17, 6, 6] may count 17 major structures (14 pyramids + 3 temples). Section 8 contains palla (princess) = the Acllahuasi. Gaffron collection (Munich), Nordenskiold (1925). Companion: UR1096 (numerical). Cadastral twin: UR1087. OKR field note: 'No numerical interpretation is possible... 23 different knot cluster orders' = TEXT khipu confirmed. 72 STRING (88% hits), 122 INT, 16 EMPTY.
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION (v3, 2026-04-15)
  212 cords (L1: 146, L2: 63, L3: 1, L4: 2). Architecture: 0-SEUL with subsidiaries (up to 11 per pendant).
  Monochrome B (brown) = 209/212 (1 MB, 1 VB, 1 unknown). 10 main sections.
  Provenance: Pachacamac. Collector: Eduard Gaffron (German doctor, Lima, 1882-1923), sold Munich 1907.
  Museum: Museum Five Continents, Munich. Old ID: AS095.
  Partial (OKR: complete=0.0).
  OKR: 'No numerical interpretation is possible as the knot clusters do not conform to the base ten positional system. Twenty-three different knot cluster orders are found.' = TEXT khipu confirmed.
  OKR: 'AS095 is similar to AS087 [=UR1087] in that they both have different types of knots in all positions.'
  Also discussed by Nordenskiold (1925, Table 3).
  72 STRING (88% hits), 122 INT, 16 EMPTY.
  Companion: UR1096 (AS096, same Gaffron, NUMERICAL: 0% STRING, 60 INT, 11 groups of 6 = the accounts).
  Cadastral twin: UR1087 (AS087, same Gaffron, same 'no numerical' verdict).

DATA QUALITY (v3)
  88 percent dict hits on 72 STRING cords.
  OKR explicitly confirms TEXT status -- the decimal-positional failure IS the confirmation that ALBA syllabary applies.
  No Ascher lattice (cadastral surveys are lexical-structural).

ASCHER LAYER
  Not applicable. 'No numerical interpretation' in OKR = the knots are syllabic, not decimal-positional.

HYPOTHESIS
  UR1095 is a CADASTRAL SURVEY of the sacred precinct of Pachacamac. The scribe walked the site -- the 14 pyramids with ramps, the enclosure walls, the gorges between buildings -- and recorded each feature by name. Monochrome brown because the document serves a single function: terrain.

VOCABULARY (key markers, 72 STRING cords, 88% dict hits)
  qaqa (rock/mountain/temple platform x13) -- DOMINANT, naming platforms and huacas.
  siqa (to climb/ramp x5) -- the PIRAMIDES CON RAMPA (14 at Pachacamac).
  chaqa (gorge/ravine x4) -- gaps between buildings.
  taqa (boundary/separate x4) -- property boundaries.
  qama (to inhabit x4) -- occupancy.
  llallalla (lla + llalla, to deceive x3) -- contested claims.
  kaqa (wall/cliff-AY x3) -- enclosure walls.
  waqa (huaca x2) -- sacred sites.
  piqa (who? x2) -- formal interrogation.
  paki (to break x2) -- damage.
  wana (to correct/punish x1), chaki (foot/Scorpio x1), mama (mother x1), chiqa (truth x1).
  qapa (noble x1), palla (princess/noble woman x1), qata (roof x1), paqa (dawn/east x1).
  Domain: 17/25 glossed words = GEOGRAPHY (68%). Pure cadastral.

STRUCTURE -- 10 SECTIONS
  Section 1 (1 cord): Opening -- qaqa (rock) = site title.
  Section 2 (4 cords): INT HEADER [17, 17, 6, 6] -- possibly 17 major structures (14 pyramids + 3 temples).
  Section 3 (41 cords): MAIN SURVEY -- the precinct walk-through. 3x siqa = 3 pyramids in central sector.
  Section 4 (20 cords): SECONDARY SECTOR -- chiqa (truth/right) = legal survey marker.
  Section 5 (33 cords): OCCUPANCY INQUIRY -- piqa... piqa... qama = 'Who? Who? Inhabits...'
  Section 6 (7 cords): Numerical summary.
  Section 7 (26 cords): REVIEW -- return to geographic features. qapa (noble) = elite sector.
  Section 8 (11 cords): CONCLUSION -- palla (princess) = the Acllahuasi sector.
  Section 9 (1 cord): Final entry with 11 subsidiaries.
  Section 10 (5 cords): TAIL -- empty cords (closing).

HISTORICAL CONTEXT
  Pachacamac (Pacha Kamaq = 'Earth Creator'): Peru's most important oracle, 600+ hectares, 32 km south of Lima. 14 piramides con rampa = stepped platforms with monumental ramps (Late Horizon 1450-1533). The Acllahuasi housed the acllakuna (chosen women). A dedicated 'House of the Quipus' stored administrative records.
  Gaffron: German physician in Lima 1882-1923, sold collection to Munich 1907/1909.
  Nordenskiold (1925) noted non-conformity to decimal system, speculated 'prophecies and horoscopes.' Our reading: a land survey.

KEY PASSAGES
  Section 2: header [17, 17, 6, 6] -- if 17 = major structures at Pachacamac. Verifiable.
  Section 3: siqa x3 -- three ramp-climbs in main precinct.
  Section 4: chiqa -- legal survey marker, boundary officially recorded.
  Section 5: piqa... piqa... -- occupancy investigation with conflict hints.
  Section 8: palla -- princess / Acllahuasi sector.

STALE v1 CLAIMS SUPERSEDED
  - Nordenskiold 1925's speculation that UR1095 records 'prophecies and horoscopes' -- DISCARDED. v3 reading identifies this as a cadastral land-survey of Pachacamac.

PRESERVED v1 OBSERVATIONS
  - 212 cords, monochrome brown, 10 sections.
  - Gaffron collection Munich.
  - Companion with UR1096 (numerical) and UR1087 (cadastral twin).
  - Non-conformity to decimal system (OKR + Nordenskiold): preserved as CONFIRMATION of text status.
narrative:
Rock. That is the first word -- *qaqa*, cord 1, one syllable repeated twice, naming the temple platform where the survey begins. At Pachacamac, everything begins with rock: the Temple of the Sun is rock and adobe, the Temple of Pachacamac is rock and paint, and the fourteen pyramids with ramps are rock and plaster rising from the desert floor.

The header counts the structures: 17, 17, 6, 6. If seventeen is the number of major buildings -- fourteen pyramids plus the three great temples -- the scribe has inventoried the site before he walks it.

Then he walks. Section 3, forty-one cords, the longest in the khipu: *qaqa* (rock), *chaqa* (gorge), *taqa* (boundary), *kaqa* (wall, in Aymara because this is the coast where both languages meet). Then *siqa* -- to climb. A ramp. The first of five ramps in the survey, and Pachacamac has fourteen pyramids with ramps. The scribe climbs, notes the boundary, climbs again -- *siqa*, *taqa*, *siqa*, *siqa*. Three ramps in the central precinct, matching the archaeological plan of the main sector.

Past the correction area -- *wana*, to punish -- to the mother's dwelling: *mama*, *qama* (to inhabit). Past the huaca: *waqa* -- the sacred place, named twice in the walk-through. Along another wall of Aymara stone (*kaqa*), past another rock platform (*qaqa*), the surveyor records what he sees.

In section 4, the legal marker appears: *chiqa* -- truth, right, the word used for official verification. This boundary is now legally registered. The uncle's name follows in the oblique case -- *kakana*, at or for the uncle -- because a lineage holder is responsible for this sector.

Section 5 turns to interrogation. *Piqa* -- who? *Piqa* -- who? Two consecutive cords, the formal question asked twice: who inhabits this sector? Then *qama* (to inhabit), *taka* (to strike), *paki* (to break). The survey has found something wrong. Someone deceived (*llalla*), someone struck, something was broken. The surveyor is no longer just describing -- he is investigating.

The review (section 7) names a noble: *qapa*. An elite sector, with another ramp (*siqa*), another huaca (*waqa*). The surveyor has circled back.

And in the final section, a single word changes the register: *palla* -- princess. The noble woman, the chosen one. At Pachacamac, the Acllahuasi -- the House of the Chosen Women -- housed the *acllakuna* who wove for the sun god. The surveyor has reached the women's precinct, and he names it with the word for its highest-ranking inhabitant.

The khipu is monochrome brown -- 209 of 212 cords. No color code, because the document serves a single function: terrain. Brown for the earth, brown for the survey, brown for the only substantial text record ever recovered from the greatest oracle site in the Andes.

prior_work.claim: Pachacamac (Pacha Kamaq = 'Earth Creator') is Peru's most important oracle site, 600+ hectares, 32 km south of Lima. The precinct contains 14 piramides con rampa (stepped platforms with monumental ramps, Late Horizon 1450-1533), the Acllahuasi (House of the Chosen Women), and a documented 'House of the Quipus.'
prior_work.citation: Rostworowski, M. (1992). Pachacamac y el Senor de los Milagros; Eeckhout, P. (2000). The Palaces of the Lords of Ychsma: Pyramids with Ramps at Pachacamac.

prior_work.claim: UR1095 was collected by Eduard Gaffron (German doctor in Lima 1882-1923) and sold to Munich in 1907. Old ID: AS095.
prior_work.citation: Museum Five Continents Munich catalogue (Gaffron collection records).

prior_work.claim: Nordenskiold (1925, Table 3) discussed UR1095 and noted the khipu did not conform to the decimal positional system, speculating 'prophecies and horoscopes.'
prior_work.citation: Nordenskiold, E. (1925). The Secret of the Peruvian Quipus. Stockholm.

prior_work.claim: OKR field note: 'No numerical interpretation is possible as the knot clusters do not conform to the base ten positional system. Twenty-three different knot cluster orders are found.' AND 'AS095 is similar to AS087 [=UR1087] in that they both have different types of knots in all positions.'
prior_work.citation: Open Khipu Repository cord-notes for UR1095 / AS095 (Urton).

v3_contribution.claim: UR1095 RECLASSIFIED from Nordenskiold's 'prophecies and horoscopes' speculation to CADASTRAL SURVEY of the sacred precinct of Pachacamac. Basis: 17 of 25 glossed words = geography domain (68 percent); siqa x5 direct match to 14 piramides con rampa architecture; palla in section 8 matching the Acllahuasi sector; section-2 header [17, 17, 6, 6] matching the 14-pyramids + 3-temples architectural count.

The OKR 'no numerical interpretation' observation is REINTERPRETED as CONFIRMATION of TEXT status: the 23 different knot cluster orders are SYLLABIC (ALBA) not decimal (accounting). The non-conformity is not a defect -- it is the signature of a text khipu.

evidence.test: Knot cluster order variation count vs decimal-positional constraint

v3_contribution.claim: Section-3 walk-through (41 cords) demonstrates the surveyor's route: qaqa -> chaqa -> taqa -> kaqa -> siqa -> taqa -> siqa -> siqa -> wana -> qaqa -> chaki -> chaqa -> mama -> qama -> qaqa -> waqa -> kaqa -> qaqa. Rock -> gorge -> boundary -> wall -> climb -> boundary -> climb -> climb -> ... The scribe's physical footpath through the precinct is reconstructible.
UR1095 is the ONLY substantial TEXT khipu recovered from Pachacamac (35 percent STRING -- highest among 20+ Pachacamac khipus in the corpus). All other Pachacamac khipus are numerical or mixed.

evidence.test: STRING percentage ranking across Pachacamac-provenance khipus

v3_contribution.claim: Section-8 palla = princess / Acllahuasi sector: the scribe closes the survey by naming the Acllahuasi with the term for its highest-ranking inhabitants. Matches the documented spatial organisation of Pachacamac precinct (Acllahuasi on the periphery, surveyed after the main pyramids).

companions.note: Cadastral twin in the Gaffron collection (AS087). Same 'no numerical interpretation' OKR verdict (both khipus have different knot types in all positions, confirming text status). Shares 7 geographic terms with UR1095. Together UR1095 + UR1087 = the two cadastral texts of the Gaffron collection.

Numerical companion in the Gaffron collection (AS096, same collector). 0 percent STRING, 60 INT, 11 groups of 6 = the accounts accompanying UR1095's survey. Together UR1095 (text survey) + UR1096 (numerical accounts) = the Pachacamac cadastral dossier.

Pachacamac ceque-line huaca registry. UR1095 is the PHYSICAL MAP of the precinct; UR203 is the SACRED MAP of its ceque system. Together with UR193 (oracle consultations), the three form the Pachacamac trilogy: physical / sacred / oracular.
Pachacamac oracle consultation register. UR1095 records the precinct's physical layout; UR193 records the consultations that take place within it.

Incahuasi cadastral survey (UE 17). Parallel site-specific cadastre at a different major Inca administrative centre. UR1095 has siqa x5 (ramps, unique to Pachacamac); UR277 lacks siqa (no pyramids at Incahuasi). The absence/presence of siqa is a SITE-ARCHITECTURE DIAGNOSTIC.

word_reading.note: 88 percent dict hits on 72 STRING cords. 17 of 25 glossed words (68 percent) in the geography domain. Core cadastral vocabulary (qaqa, siqa, chaqa, taqa, kaqa, qama, piqa, waqa) all at 100 percent.

document_type.note: Cadastral_survey signature fires at full strength: 7 of 8 canonical markers + the siqa = ramps / piramides-con-rampa direct match to Pachacamac's documented architecture (14 pyramids). Section-8 palla matches the Acllahuasi sector. OKR+Nordenskiold confirm text status via decimal-positional non-conformity.

narrative.note: Internally coherent (qaqa site opening -> [17,17,6,6] header counts structures -> 41-cord precinct walk-through -> chiqa legal marker -> piqa occupancy interrogation -> qapa elite sector -> palla Acllahuasi closing). Externally corroborated by Pachacamac's documented architecture (Rostworowski, Eeckhout) and by the Gaffron/Nordenskiold provenance chain.

data_integrity.note: OKR+KFG agreement preserved on 212 cords. Monochrome brown (209/212) simplifies the reading. No Ascher layer needed. Partial-condition flag in OKR (complete=0.0) but the readable sections form a coherent cadastral narrative.

sections.title: Site Title
sections.reading: Single opening cord: qaqa -- the rock. The survey opens at the rock platform (likely the Temple of Pachacamac or the Temple of the Sun). The site is named before the structures are counted.

key_cords.note: FIRST WORD -- site title: the rock platform where the survey begins

sections.title: Structural Header
sections.reading: INT header [17, 17, 6, 6]. If 17 = number of major structures, this counts the 14 pyramids with ramps + 3 great temples at Pachacamac. The scribe inventories before he walks.

sections.title: Main Precinct Walk-Through
sections.reading: The LONGEST section (41 cords): the surveyor's physical route. qaqa -> chaqa -> taqa -> kaqa -> siqa -> taqa -> siqa -> siqa -> wana -> qaqa -> chaki -> chaqa -> mama -> qama -> qaqa -> waqa -> kaqa -> qaqa. Three siqa = three ramp-climbs, matching the central sector's pyramid-cluster architecture. The scribe walks from rock to gorge, past boundaries, along walls, up ramps.

key_cords.note: FIRST RAMP -- first pyramid's ramp climbed
THIRD RAMP -- three ramps in central sector
huaca -- sacred place named in the walk

sections.title: Secondary Sector + Legal Marker
sections.reading: 20 cords. chiqa (truth/right) at the centre -- the legal survey marker. This boundary is now officially registered. Followed by kakana (at/for the uncle) -- lineage-holder identification for this sector.

key_cords.note: LEGAL SURVEY MARKER -- boundary officially registered

sections.title: Occupancy Inquiry
sections.reading: 33 cords. The survey turns to INTERROGATION. piqa... piqa... qama (Who? Who? Inhabits...). Then taka (strike) and paki (break). Someone deceived (llalla), struck, broke something. The survey has found something wrong.

sections.title: Numerical Summary
sections.reading: 7 cords numerical, summarising the inquiry's findings quantitatively.

sections.title: Elite-Sector Review
sections.reading: 26 cords reviewing the elite sectors. qapa (noble) appears -- the sector's administrator or occupant is a ranked noble. Another siqa (ramp) returns (a pyramid in this sector), another waqa (huaca).

sections.title: Acllahuasi Conclusion
sections.reading: 11 cords. palla (princess / noble woman) = the Acllahuasi sector. The House of the Chosen Women closes the survey -- the acllakuna who wove for the sun god at Pachacamac.

key_cords.note: PRINCESS -- the Acllahuasi (House of the Chosen Women) sector

sections.title: Final Multi-Subsidiary Entry
sections.reading: Single cord with 11 L2 subsidiaries -- a compound record detailing the Acllahuasi's complex sub-structure (multiple acllakuna cohorts? multiple weaving halls?).

sections.title: Tail / Closure
sections.reading: Remaining cords include L2+ subsidiaries and the closing tail (empty cords). Document formally closes.

references: Sivan, J. (2026). The Khipu as a Layered Information System. ALBA Project preprint.
